title: Marduk theory
wikitext:
[]: Marduk theory.             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Mon Sep 20, 2010 4:26 pm I'm sorry if this thread already existed, I searched for it, but found nothing. Onto the subject! I was having history class. It was about the neolitihcal revolution, the first cities in the middle east and such. The people believed in the excistence of many gods, but they had one leader de facto, sorta like Jupiter. Suprisingly enough, the name of this upergod turned out to be Marduk. Shocked as I was I developed my own little theory. Marduk(The institute) selects children to become pilots of mand-made gods called EVA's. Yet, the Marduk institue turns out to be an organ of SEELE. Since MARDUK's the Supreme god of the Babylonians, he's the most important guy. And lesser gods are of course in excistence as well. NGE's MARDUK on the other hand, has the lesser gods as to. However, SEELE's controlling MARDUK, and with that, they technically state to control the supremegod and all below them. Knowing SEELE, this all fits in very well, IMO. If I haven't been writing this too shabby, and if you can understand this, please let me know what you think.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Sep 20, 2010 7:17 pm There's a bit more info about it in the wiki that might interest you: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Marduk_Institute

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 20, 2010 8:48 pm The Marduk is more or less a Nerv fabrication, having little to due with Seele. Since it's Nerv that has direct control of the Evas, they would be the ones picking the pilots as well.

[]:             Quote Post by loyal servant » Mon Sep 20, 2010 10:30 pm something that has always made me wonder is, who was the lady who told Kaji all that info (at the abandoned warehouse) and what was her connection to NERV and SEELE...

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Sep 20, 2010 10:48 pm The Fourth Evangelion wrote: Since MARDUK's the Supreme god of the Babylonians, he's the most important guy. And lesser gods are of course in excistence as well. NGE's MARDUK on the other hand, has the lesser gods as to. However, SEELE's controlling MARDUK, and with that, they technically state to control the supremegod and all below them.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Sep 20, 2010 11:39 pm loyal servant wrote: something that has always made me wonder is, who was the lady who told Kaji all that info (at the abandoned warehouse) and what was her connection to NERV and SEELE...

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 21, 2010 12:14 am Sachi wrote: There's probably a thread for this, but she one of Kaji's informants, I believe with the Japanese government. There's a theory that suggests that she's Ritsuko's grandmother.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Sep 21, 2010 12:17 am Azathoth wrote: Seele to kidnap Ritsuko

[]:             Quote Post by loyal servant » Tue Sep 21, 2010 2:20 am Sachi wrote: There's probably a thread for this, but she one of Kaji's informants, I believe with the Japanese government. There's a theory that suggests that she's Ritsuko's grandmother.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Sep 21, 2010 5:31 am Marduk is Gendo Ikari.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Sep 21, 2010 8:03 am loyal servant wrote: Interesting... If I recall correctly, she was feeding cats in that scene, this theory could be valid. Gendo does send Ritsuko to Seele, but the question is, did he do that because he knew her grandmother was involved (remember, they knew what Kaji was up to) and Seele probably had a clue, hence taking the heat off of Kaji just a little while longer?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Sep 21, 2010 9:26 am loyal servant wrote: Interesting... If I recall correctly, she was feeding cats in that scene, this theory could be valid. Gendo does send Ritsuko to Seele, but the question is, did he do that because he knew her grandmother was involved (remember, they knew what Kaji was up to) and Seele probably had a clue, hence taking the heat off of Kaji just a little while longer?

[]:             Quote Post by loyal servant » Tue Sep 21, 2010 10:11 am Sachi wrote: Kaji was already dead at this point. Gendo sent Ritsuko in place of Rei because he didn't want Seele to question Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Tue Sep 21, 2010 10:50 am loyal servant wrote: On the same token, she knew Kaji was up to mischief and didn't dob him in, or atleast I don't think she did... Interesting point regarding the phone call though, I mean, Anno doesn't put pointless stuff into the dialogue as filler. If anything, maybe he put that in as a clue to Kaji's demise?

[]:             Quote Post by loyal servant » Tue Sep 21, 2010 11:18 am Based on that explanation, Id say you're fair spot on!!

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Sep 21, 2010 11:36 am thewayneiac wrote: However, if I wanted to try to prove that Cat = Kaji, I would try to reconcile it with Ep. 24, where the cat comes up again. In the scene where Gendo is interrogating Ritsuko in her cell, he asks "Why did you destroy the Dummy System?", and she starts talking about her dead cat that her grandmother had been caring for. If the phone call was in code and they were really talking about Kaji (i.e. nothing actually happened to a cat), then she must be talking about Kaji here too. On the other hand, if she's really talking about a cat here, it was a real cat in her phone call with her grandmother as well; I don't see a way you can have it both ways. So it would strengthen the case if someone could come up with a reason why she would bring up Kaji to Gendo and why it would still be in code. If you are passing secrets on the phone to a spy there's every reason to speak in code, but Gendo presumably knows all about Kaji.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Sep 21, 2010 1:53 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I guess the next question would be, "How does Kaji's death cause Ritsuko to destroy the Dummy Plug system?" I guess emotional instability, maybe, since I'm not sure of destroying the Dummy System was something Kaji was aiming for, leading up to a "Kaji would want this" action from Ritsuko. Even if it was caused by emotional instability from Kaji's death, it would make a little more sense than emotional instability from the death of a cat. But I'm more inclined it was more because she was stripped nude in front of fapping monoliths.

[]:             Quote Post by The Fourth Evangelion » Wed Sep 22, 2010 11:11 am Ornette wrote: There's a bit more info about it in the wiki that might interest you: http://wiki.evageeks.org/Marduk_Institute

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Sep 22, 2010 12:17 pm What if the trinket didn't give Kaji's investigations away at all? I think Gendo (and Ritsuko) were aware of Kaji's second job right from the start, and this was just Ritsuko honestly trying to warn him off without betraying her own status in the secret goings-on. Thus grandma is not a spy and the cat conversation is merely an interesting parallel, not a literal code. When she tells Gendo about it she's making a point about how little he cares about her outside of Nerv and also trying to talk around her rape, which she never brings herself to openly admit (and thus Gendo really has no idea whatsoever why she ruined the dummies).

[]:             Quote Post by Aozora » Wed Sep 22, 2010 6:07 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I guess the next question would be, "How does Kaji's death cause Ritsuko to destroy the Dummy Plug system?" I guess emotional instability, maybe, since I'm not sure of destroying the Dummy System was something Kaji was aiming for, leading up to a "Kaji would want this" action from Ritsuko. Even if it was caused by emotional instability from Kaji's death, it would make a little more sense than emotional instability from the death of a cat. But I'm more inclined it was more because she was stripped nude in front of fapping monoliths.
